reasoning: C wins on rigor and calibration: it is the only response that runs an explicit quantitative disconfirming test (a ~15-17% capacity bump vs ~4% population over two years that 'does not arithmetically close'), names a falsifiable check list, and states a calibrated confidence (~85%) while flagging the peripheral-bypass exception. B matches C on structural depth (Meadows leverage hierarchy, reinforcing vs balancing loop, selective-skeptic disconfirming test) and bias awareness ('cause is out there' deflection) but its arithmetic is qualitative rather than worked. D is the most polished and actionable (cites Duranton-Turner 2011, concrete implementation steps) but does less to surface its own cognitive traps and steelman population growth, and its sanity-check is lighter. A is correct and clear but the shallowest: it offers a vaguer 10-20% elasticity, no structural loop framing, weaker disconfirmation, and thinner bias work, so it ranks last.
